Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a product available for purchase in the Global Cart application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the product.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a category to which products can belong.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

ShoppingCart.title: ShoppingCart
ShoppingCart.description: Represents a shopping cart for a user, holding references to products.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ShoppingCart entity.

productIds.description: References to Products in the cart. (Relationship: ShoppingCart 1:N Product)

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Global Cart application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

location.description: The user's location to determine currency and payment provider.

Payment.title: Payment
Payment.description: Represents a payment transaction processed via ToyyibPay or PayPal.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Payment entity.

currency.description: The currency used for the payment (e.g., MYR, USD).

paymentGateway.description: The payment gateway used (e.g., ToyyibPay, PayPal).

transactionId.description: The transaction ID from the payment gateway.

definition.description: Stores categories to which products belong.
Stores product information, including name, description, image URL, price, category ID, and tags.
Stores the shopping cart for each user, including references to product IDs.

params.name: shoppingCartId
params.description: Unique identifier for the shopping cart.

definition.description: Stores payment transaction information for each user, including amount, currency, payment gateway, transaction ID, and timestamp.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support the Global Cart application's core features, focusing on product listings, shopping carts, and dynamic payment gateway selection (ToyyibPay or PayPal). The structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and supports secure list operations.

*   `/categories`: Stores category data. Flat structure simplifies querying and management.
*   `/products`: Stores product data. Flat structure simplifies querying and management. Includes `categoryId` for filtering.
*   `/users/{userId}/shopping_cart`: Each user has their own shopping cart subcollection. This ensures only the user can access and modify their cart. Product IDs are stored in an array within the shopping cart document.
*   `/users/{userId}/payments`: Stores payment history for each user.  This allows users to view their past transactions.

This structure ensures Authorization Independence because access to user-specific data (shopping carts, payments) is determined by the `userId` in the path, not by querying other documents. QAPs are supported as listing products or categories does not require special security rules.  User-specific data is segregated under the `/users/{userId}` path.
